title: ATDD prompt followed faithfully: acceptance tests drive the system through the MCP protocol only
evidence: tests/acceptance/harness.ts:30 startSystem() connects a real MCP Client to the real server over InMemoryTransport; tests/acceptance/competition-queries.test.ts:11 beforeEach starts a fresh empty system per scenario
suggestion: None — this is the prompt-conformance signal worth recording, not a defect.

title: Unit TDD underpins the acceptance layer as the prompt asks
evidence: tests/unit/normalize.test.ts and tests/unit/loaders.test.ts exercise internals (5 tests) beneath the 29 acceptance tests
suggestion: None — confirms the 'finer-grained unit TDD underneath' instruction was honored.

title: Player nationality and position filters use exact equality, not substring
evidence: src/data/store.ts:1191 `foldText(p.nationality ?? '') !== nationality` and store.ts:1193 position use `!==`, unlike name/club which use includes()
suggestion: Acceptable for 'Brazil'/'ST', but a query like nationality='Brazilian' or a partial position would miss; document or relax to startsWith if partial matching is desired.

title: Index is fully rebuilt whenever a match is added
evidence: src/data/store.ts:833 addMatch sets this.index = null; build() (store.ts:846) is O(n) two-pass over all rawMatches
suggestion: Fine for batch-load-then-query (the production and test path); interleaving addMatch after queries would trigger repeated full rebuilds. Note only.

title: Same-base-name clubs across states only disambiguated when ambiguity is observed in the data
evidence: src/data/store.ts:893 canonicalKey keeps a state suffix only when teamKey is in ambiguousBases; src/domain/normalize.ts:417 comment acknowledges the limitation
suggestion: Documented heuristic; a curated club registry would resolve fully. Out of scope for the demo dataset.

title: Top scorers (TASK §4) not implemented — not in pinned requirements and not inferable from data
evidence: TASK.md:231 lists 'Top scorers (if inferable from data)'; FIFA player CSV has no goals-scored column and match CSVs have no scorer attribution, so it is not derivable
suggestion: No action — correctly omitted; REQUIREMENTS.json does not include it.
